text: Viasat's investigation identified a ground-based network intrusion in which an attacker exploited a misconfiguration in a VPN appliance to gain remote access to the trusted management segment of the KA-SAT network. The attacker then moved laterally through this trusted management network to a specific segment used to manage and operate the network, and used that access to execute legitimate, targeted management commands on a large number of residential modems simultaneously. Viasat also reported high volumes of focused malicious traffic from SurfBeam2 and SurfBeam 2+ modems and associated customer premises equipment, producing a targeted denial-of-service condition and causing large numbers of modems across Europe to exit the network.
